title: Mark/index dislocation alert research report
summary: Perpetual futures use mark price and index price mechanics for risk and liquidation accounting. A persistent or rapidly expanding mark/index dislocation can identify exchange-specific stress, forced positioning or data quality risk. The strategy should start as an alert and risk filter for carry, premium and momentum candidates rather than an execution signal.
claims: Mark price and index price are separate market data surfaces, so dislocation can be measured directly without relying only on trade-derived candles.
Dislocation is more useful as a stress and quality filter than as a standalone trade, because adverse selection and liquidation mechanics can dominate short windows.
The signal can improve existing funding, basis, OI momentum and premium z-score candidates by suppressing entries during abnormal mark/index divergence.
formulas: compression_flag = abs(dislocation_bps_t) < abs(dislocation_bps_t_minus_1) after abs(dislocation_zscore) exceeds threshold
cost_items: No direct execution cost when used as alert or filter.
If later used for directional entry, include taker fee, slippage, funding paid during holding period and opportunity cost from filtered trades.
False positives can suppress otherwise valid carry or momentum candidates.
failure_modes: Dislocation reflects real exchange stress rather than a reversible pricing error.
Index price source or mark calculation changes across exchanges create non-comparable signals.
Extreme windows have poor fill quality and larger slippage than replay assumptions.
Thresholds overfit one liquidation event or one venue's mark-price behavior.
evidence_notes: Operator fit: high as an alert or risk filter; low as a standalone automated entry signal until execution quality is proven.
Data boundary: Binance exposes mark price, mark price kline and index price kline endpoints; other exchanges need venue-specific mapping before cross-exchange comparison.
Safety boundary: this artifact does not permit live trading, production config changes or auto-deployment.
